SCHOOL::Art 7 (Ever Ancient, Ever New, Level 2) Syllabus::Read Unit 5, Chapter 20, The Rococo.

SCHOOL::History & Geog 8 (Old World & America) Syllabus::Write the best part of the story you just finished reading. If possible write this in the first person as if you were one of the characters in the story. Just tell that part of the story as if you were telling it to a friend, but do it in writing. If this is too difficult, then you can write the best part of the story in the third person as a narration.

SCHOOL::Poetry 8 syllabus::Work on the next 6 lines, up to “... stones and floods,” and review the previous lines. Review the poems you learned in First Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of these lesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, that is fine, just read and enjoy them.

SCHOOL::Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church) Syllabus::Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the corporal works of mercy. Then write a short summary of the various kinds of law, which constitute the external guide of the moral law
SCHOOL::Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus::Do the Lesson 12 exercises. Go over any Lesson 12 Key Words you had trouble spelling

SCHOOL::Beginning Latin III::Lesson 22. Review distinguishing between helping verbs and "to be" verbs (lesson XX). Read the twenty-second lesson. Oral Drill:decline the adjective "magnus, a, um" and the noun "dux, ducis." Then put them together, remembering that "dux" is a masculinethird declension word. Do the Day 1 exercises. Practice vocabulary for ten minutes.Oral Drill Answer Key:m: magnus, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magni, magnorum, magnis, magnos, magnisf: magna, magnae, magnae, magnam, magna, magnae, magnarum, magnis, magnas, magnisn: magnum, magni, magno, magnum, magno, magna, magnorum, magnis, magna, magnisdux, ducis, duci, ducem, duce, duces, ducum, ducibus, duces, ducibusmagnus dux, magni ducis, magno duci, magnum ducem, magno duce, magni duces, magnorumducum, magnis ducibus,magnos duces, magnis ducibus
SCHOOL::English 8::Do the Verb Review today, pages 108-110. (T.E. pages 206-211.)

SCHOOL::History & Geog 7::Locate a good map of the Mediterranean Area. Trace (or draw freehand) the outline of Italy, including the Mediterranean region. Themaps on pp. 14-19 of Historical Atlas of the World by Rand McNally are all good maps for this. Photocopy it 6 times. Also, read TheOld World and America pp. 62-73.

SCHOOL::Religion 7::Discuss the questions for Chapter 23. Or, if you prefer, you may write out your answers.1. What device did Paul use to deflect interest from himself?2. How did Paul escape from the plot of the Jews?3. Where was Paul sent?4. Did the governor hear Paul's case immediately?Review your new cards, both the questions and answers and the Bible verses. Also review three cards you worked on previously.

SCHOOL::Spelling & Vocabulary 8::Look at the Familiar Words and Challenge Words for each root word in Lesson 11. If there are any you do not know, or can't figureout, look them up. You can add them to your notebook. Go over any of the Key Words you had trouble spelling.

If this is too difficult, then you can write the best part of the story in the third person as a narration.
SCHOOL::Poetry 8 syllabus::Work on the next 6 lines, up to “... their blood,” and review the previous lines.

SCHOOL::Religion 8 (Our Life in the Church) Syllabus::Work for 5 minutes or less reviewing the corporal works of mercy. Then write a short summary of the various kinds of law, which constitute the external guide of the moral law
SCHOOL::Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus::Do the Lesson 12 exercises. Go over any Lesson 12 Key Words you had trouble spelling

SCHOOL::Poetry 8 Syllabus::Start memorizing “The Merchant of Venice”, Act V, Scene I, lines 53-88 (this passage begins with "How sweet the moonlight sleeps upon this bank..."). Spend five minutes each day this week working on the first 4 lines, from “How sweet the moonlight sleeps…” to “...sweet harmony.” Review the poems you learned in Seventh Grade (see the Poetry List for Kindergarten to Eighth Grade found at the end of these lesson plans). Recite as many as you can from memory. If you have not already memorized these poems or cannot recite them, that is fine, just read and enjoy them.

SCHOOL::Religion 7 (Acts of the Apostles) Syllabus::Discuss the questions for Chapter 26, or write out your answers. What kind of Jew was Paul? What did King Agrippa think of Paul’s account? Practice defending your faith. Have someone ask you one or more of the questions you have been working on in your Friendly Defender cards. Answer the question and give the Bible verse that pertains to that answer.

SCHOOL::Spelling & Vocabulary 8 (Vocabulary from Classical Roots B) Syllabus::Take a spelling test on Lesson 11 Key words. If there are definitions you are unsure of, read them over again.
